title: GraspSearchResponse
description: grasp-search service -> BFF output. Top-N ranking with score breakdown. Wire form uses camelCase.

candidates.description: Ranked candidates, rank ascending (1 = best). Not a single best pose.

pose.description: The pose the solver *decided*, as a closed kind-discriminated union. Only solver-decided facts ride the wire; presentation (approach vector, ghost color, gripper-width display, animation) is derived client-side from the frame + convention and is NOT part of this contract. The only intentional growth point is adding a new kind (which bumps contractVersion). kind is the discriminator; each branch is closed (additionalProperties:false). 'optional' is not a design word here -- field presence is implied by kind.

poseEndEffector.description: End-effector (hand/tool) pose expressed in a base/world frame. Cartesian.

poseJointSpace.description: Pose expressed in the joint space of a named kinematic chain.

chainRef.description: Which kinematic chain (robot) the joint values belong to.

cartesianFrame.description: A rigid-body pose in a base/world frame.

scoreBreakdown.description: Per-candidate score breakdown explaining why a pose ranked where it did.

objectiveScores.description: objective name -> normalized 0-1 value (absolute basis, so scores are comparable across requests).

totalScore.description: Weighted sum of the normalized objective scores.
